chapter: Chapter 8: Force and Laws of Motion

questions.question: What is force?
questions.answer: Force is a push or pull acting on an object.

questions.question: What can force do to an object?
questions.answer: Force can change the speed, direction, or shape of an object.

questions.question: What are balanced forces?
questions.answer: Balanced forces are equal forces acting in opposite directions.

questions.question: What happens when balanced forces act on an object?
questions.answer: There is no change in the state of motion of the object.

questions.question: What are unbalanced forces?
questions.answer: Unbalanced forces are forces that cause a change in motion of an object.

questions.question: What is inertia?
questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist change in its state of motion or rest.

questions.question: What does inertia depend on?
questions.answer: Inertia depends on the mass of an object.

questions.question: State Newton’s First Law of Motion.
questions.answer: An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an unbalanced force.

questions.question: What is the first law of motion also called?
questions.answer: It is called the Law of Inertia.

questions.question: What is momentum?
questions.answer: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity.

questions.question: Write the formula for momentum.
questions.answer: Momentum = mass × velocity (p = mv).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of momentum?
questions.answer: kg m/s.

questions.question: State Newton’s Second Law of Motion.
questions.answer: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the applied force.
key_points: force proportional to change in momentum

questions.question: Write the formula for force.
questions.answer: Force = mass × acceleration (F = ma).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.answer: Newton (N).

questions.question: What is one Newton?
questions.answer: One Newton is the force that produces acceleration of 1 m/s² in a mass of 1 kg.

questions.question: State Newton’s Third Law of Motion.
questions.answer: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

questions.question: Do action and reaction act on the same object?
questions.answer: No, they act on different objects.

questions.question: Give one example of Newton’s Third Law.
questions.answer: A gun recoils backward when a bullet is fired forward.

questions.question: Why do passengers fall forward when a moving bus stops suddenly?
questions.answer: Due to inertia of motion.

questions.question: Which force opposes motion between surfaces?
questions.answer: Friction.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of mass?
questions.answer: Kilogram (kg).

questions.question: Which quantity measures inertia?
questions.answer: Mass.

questions.question: Name the three laws of motion.
questions.answer: Newton’s First, Second, and Third Laws of Motion.

questions.question: Who proposed the laws of motion?
questions.answer: Sir Isaac Newton.
